text.title: LlamaIndex: Production Deployment and Evaluation
text.summary: Production settings, index persistence, caching, observability, evaluation, and deployment gotchas.

rule.statement: Always persist indexes to disk with storage_context.persist() and reload with load_index_from_storage() on startup; never rebuild from documents on every process start.
rule.rationale: Rebuilding an index from scratch calls the embedding API for every document. On 10K+ documents this costs hundreds of dollars and takes minutes. Persisted indexes reload in seconds with no API cost.

example.code:
from llama_index.core import StorageContext, load_index_from_storage import os
INDEX_DIR = "./storage"
def get_or_build_index(docs_dir: str): if os.path.exists(INDEX_DIR): storage_context = StorageContext.from_defaults(persist_dir=INDEX_DIR) return load_index_from_storage(storage_context) documents = SimpleDirectoryReader(docs_dir).load_data() index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents, show_progress=True) index.storage_context.persist(persist_dir=INDEX_DIR) return index

rule.statement: Add CallbackManager with LlamaDebugHandler in development and LangfuseCallbackHandler in production to trace all LLM inputs/outputs, token usage, and retrieval events.
rule.rationale: Without tracing, debugging retrieval failures and hallucinations requires guesswork. Langfuse provides persistent traces with latency breakdown per component, making cost and quality optimization data-driven.

example.code:
from llama_index.core.callbacks import CallbackManager from llama_index.callbacks.langfuse import LangfuseCallbackHandler
langfuse_handler = LangfuseCallbackHandler( public_key=os.environ["LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY"], secret_key=os.environ["LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY"], ) Settings.callback_manager = CallbackManager([langfuse_handler])

rule.statement: Run FaithfulnessEvaluator and RelevancyEvaluator against a labeled QA dataset before deploying; target faithfulness score above 0.9 and relevancy above 0.85.
rule.rationale: RAG does not eliminate hallucination — the LLM can still confabulate when retrieved context is ambiguous. Automated evaluation with LLM-as-judge provides a quantitative gate before each production release.

example.code:
from llama_index.core.evaluation import ( BatchEvalRunner, FaithfulnessEvaluator, RelevancyEvaluator, generate_question_context_pairs, )
qa_dataset = generate_question_context_pairs(nodes[:50], llm=Settings.llm, num_questions_per_chunk=2) runner = BatchEvalRunner( evaluators={"faithfulness": FaithfulnessEvaluator(llm=Settings.llm), "relevancy": RelevancyEvaluator(llm=Settings.llm)}, workers=4, ) eval_results = await runner.aevaluate_queries(query_engine=query_engine, queries=list(qa_dataset.queries.values()))

rule.statement: Always set Settings.embed_model explicitly; never rely on the default, which can change between versions and cause silent re-embedding at different dimensions.
rule.rationale: Index persistence format and embedding dimensions must match between build and load. A version upgrade that changes the default embedding model causes load failures or silent quality degradation.

antipattern.description: Calling from_documents() on large corpora synchronously in a request handler.
antipattern.reason: from_documents() is blocking and slow for large corpora. Embed documents asynchronously at ingestion time using async batch processing; never rebuild on request.

antipattern.description: Passing metadata_extractor=None omission when unexpected API costs appear.
antipattern.reason: Some LlamaIndex configurations call the LLM for metadata extraction by default. Disable with metadata_extractor=None unless title/keyword extraction is explicitly needed.
